[45:35]So the first issue that I want to get into is the H-1B visa scandal.

[45:40]This is something I did not get to cover on my show.

[45:55]if you've been following the news,

[46:20]And one of, in my opinion, the most important events of that period took place around Christmas

[46:44]Trump's base of conservative America-first supporters,

[46:59]And the substance of the debacle was over

[47:02]this temporary work visa program called the H-1B.

[47:08]And H1B has been very controversial for a very long time.

[47:12]What it is in effect is subsidized labor for corporations.

[47:17]There's a cap every year of 50,000 of these temporary work visas.

[47:24]And they go out to employees.

[47:29]They go out to foreign nationals, mostly from India,

[47:35]And there is a special accommodation made for them

[47:38]because they have specialized knowledge.

[47:42]That's the basis of the program.

[47:44]Every year, 50,000 of these people are brought in

[47:48]under a work visa.

[47:50]Most of them work in IT, they work in Silicon Valley,

[47:54]they work in tech.

[47:55]And again, most of them are coming from India.

[47:59]And the premise is that they are imported into the country to benefit our economy because

[48:04]they have specialized knowledge.

[48:07]The truth is they actually do not possess any kind of special knowledge.

[48:14]And although there are certain provisions that in theory are supposed to protect American

[48:27]In reality, there are many loopholes

[48:30]that corporations use to circumvent those restrictions.

[48:35]And what this is in effect,

[48:37]and it has been called this even by neoliberal economists,

[48:41]is subsidized labor.

[48:43]These are cheaper, younger, more poor employees

[48:48]from foreign countries that have a much lower standard

[48:51]of living, and they're brought in for one purpose.

[48:55]They can be hired more cheaply than American workers.

[49:01]Younger, cheaper, poorer from a country with a lower standard of living,

[49:06]and they're brought here to reduce the overall labor cost of major firms.

[50:03]one class of labor, which is unskilled labor, then this is very much the same thing, except

[50:30]whether it is low skilled or high skilled nominally,

[50:39]whether they're contributing to our country or not,

[50:52]which is to say,

[50:54]vast numbers of people are being brought here to work.

[51:23]Again, legal or illegal, low skilled or high skilled, that is their purpose.
